text.title: CR (change request) flow
text.summary: One-page CR shape, two-state lifecycle, dedicated commit prefix. Smaller than a feature, larger than a passing commit.

rule.statement: A CR file MUST fit on one page (≤80 lines) and contain three sections: Why, What changes, Optional linked-feature.
rule.rationale: Keep it cheap. If the CR needs more, it is a feature.

example.code:
# CR042: lower default rate-limit from 100/min to 60/min
linked-feature: F031
## Why Premium tier abuse logs show p95 traffic at 73/min from a single key.
## What changes - `apps/api/middleware/ratelimit.py` — default 60. - `tests/api/test_ratelimit.py` — adjust expectations. - `project-spec/non-functional.md` — update the rate-limit table.

rule.statement: CR moves through exactly two states: `crs/todo/` → `crs/done/`. No in-progress.
rule.rationale: CRs are short-lived. An extra state adds bureaucracy without surfacing anything new.

rule.statement: Commits implementing a CR MUST use prefix `cr(CR0NN): <short title>`.
rule.rationale: Lets release-notes generators split CR / BUG / FEATURE lines without parsing the body.
